math_axiom_report.kid_formalization:
================================================================================ KID-CALCULUS: MATHEMATICAL FORMALIZATION Kondensation-Information-Dichte and Assembly Index Integration ================================================================================
I. FOUNDATIONAL DEFINITIONS ================================================================================
Definition 1.1 (Ontological Condensation) ----------------------------------------- Ontological condensation $\mathcal{K}$ is the process by which information transitions from a state of maximum entropy (uniform probability distribution) to structured, causally effective configurations.
$$\mathcal{K}: \mathcal{H}_{max} \rightarrow \mathcal{H}_{structured}$$
where $\mathcal{H}_{max}$ represents the maximum entropy state and $\mathcal{H}_{structured}$ represents a lower-entropy, structured state.
Definition 1.2 (KID - Kondensation-Information-Dichte) ------------------------------------------------------ The KID measure quantifies the density of causally effective information within an interaction volume over time:
$$KID = \oint_{\partial\Omega} \frac{I_{causal}}{V_{interaction}} \, dt$$
Variable Definitions with Units: --------------------------------
| Symbol | Meaning | Units | Measurement | |--------|---------|-------|-------------| | $KID$ | Kondensation-Information-Dichte | bits·s⁻¹·m⁻³ | Derived | | $I_{causal}$ | Causally effective information | bits | Mutual information with temporal asymmetry | | $V_{interaction}$ | Volume of causal interaction | m³ | Spatial correlation volume | | $t$ | Time | s | Clock time | | $\partial\Omega$ | Closed boundary of system | - | System definition |
Dimensional Analysis: -------------------- $$[KID] = \frac{[I_{causal}]}{[V_{interaction}]} \cdot [t] = \frac{\text{bits}}{\text{m}^3} \cdot \text{s} = \text{bits} \cdot \text{s} \cdot \text{m}^{-3}$$
For time-integrated KID over duration $\tau$: $$[KID_{integrated}] = \text{bits} \cdot \text{m}^{-3}$$
Definition 1.3 (Causal Information $I_{causal}$) ----------------------------------------------- Causal information is defined as the mutual information between past and future states, weighted by causal efficacy:
$$I_{causal}(X) = I(X_{t}; X_{t+\Delta t}) \cdot \eta_{cause}$$
where: - $I(X_{t}; X_{t+\Delta t}) = H(X_{t}) + H(X_{t+\Delta t}) - H(X_{t}, X_{t+\Delta t})$ - $\eta_{cause} = \frac{P(X_{t+\Delta t} | do(X_{t}))}{P(X_{t+\Delta t})}$ (causal effect ratio)
Measurement: Via intervention studies or Granger causality in time series.
Definition 1.4 (Interaction Volume $V_{interaction}$) ---------------------------------------------------- The volume within which causal correlations are significant:
$$V_{interaction} = \int_{\mathbb{R}^3} \Theta(|C(r)| - \epsilon) \, d^3r$$
where $C(r)$ is the spatial correlation function and $\epsilon$ is a threshold (typically $\epsilon = e^{-1}$ of maximum correlation).
Measurement: Spatial correlation analysis or light-cone volume in relativistic contexts.
Axiom A1 (Information Fundamentalism) ------------------------------------- Information is the fundamental ontological substrate:
$$\forall x \in \mathcal{O}: \exists I(x) \geq 0$$
where $\mathcal{O}$ is the set of all ontological entities.
Axiom A2 (Condensation Irreversibility) ---------------------------------------- Ontological condensation is thermodynamically irreversible:
$$\mathcal{K}: \mathcal{H}_i \rightarrow \mathcal{H}_f \Rightarrow S_{total}(\mathcal{H}_f) > S_{total}(\mathcal{H}_i)$$
where $S_{total}$ includes system + environment.
Axiom A3 (Landauer Bound) -------------------------- Every condensation step has a minimum energy cost:
$$\Delta E_{min} = k_B T \ln 2 \cdot \Delta I$$
Axiom A4 (Path Dependence) --------------------------- The state of a condensed system depends on its history:
$$\Psi(t) = \mathcal{F}[\{\mathcal{K}(t')\}_{t' < t}]$$
where $\mathcal{F}$ is a functional of the condensation history.
Axiom A5 (Emergence) -------------------- New ontological levels emerge at critical KID thresholds:
$$KID > \mathcal{E}_n \Rightarrow \text{Level } n+1 \text{ emerges}$$
math_axiom_report.assembly_index_integration:
================================================================================ II. ASSEMBLY INDEX INTEGRATION ================================================================================
Definition 2.1 (Assembly Index - AI) ------------------------------------ The Assembly Index $AI(x)$ of an object $x$ is the minimum number of condensation steps required to construct $x$ from elementary building blocks:
$$AI(x) = \min_{\gamma \in \Gamma(x)} |\gamma|$$
where: - $\Gamma(x)$ is the set of all valid assembly pathways for $x$ - $|\gamma|$ is the number of steps in pathway $\gamma$ - Elementary building blocks have $AI = 0$
Definition 2.2 (Condensation Step) ---------------------------------- A condensation step $c_i$ is an irreversible operation that combines previously assembled components:
$$c_i: (x_1, x_2, ..., x_n) \rightarrow y$$
where $AI(y) = \max_j AI(x_j) + 1$
Theorem 2.1 (KID-AI Relationship) --------------------------------- For any condensed object $x$, the KID is bounded below by the Assembly Index:
$$KID(x) \geq \frac{AI(x) \cdot I_{min}}{V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{form}}$$
where: - $I_{min} = k_B T \ln 2$ (Landauer limit per bit) - $V_{eff}$ is the effective condensation volume - $\tau_{form}$ is the formation timescale
Proof Sketch: ------------ 1. Each condensation step requires at least $I_{min}$ of information processing 2. $AI(x)$ steps $\Rightarrow$ at least $AI(x) \cdot I_{min}$ information 3. Distributed over volume $V_{eff}$ and time $\tau_{form}$ 4. Therefore: $KID \geq \frac{AI(x) \cdot I_{min}}{V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{form}}$ $\square$
Theorem 2.2 (AI as Path-Integrated KID) --------------------------------------- The Assembly Index can be expressed as a path integral of KID:
$$AI(x) = \frac{1}{I_{min}} \int_{\gamma^*} KID(\vec{r}, t) \cdot V_{eff} \, dt$$
where $\gamma^*$ is the minimal KID pathway.
Proof: ------ From Theorem 2.1: $KID \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau \geq AI(x) \cdot I_{min}$
For the minimal pathway (equality condition): $$AI(x) = \frac{1}{I_{min}} \int KID \cdot V_{eff} \, dt$$
This establishes the duality: AI measures "historical depth" while KID measures "condensation intensity". $\square$
Definition 2.3 (Path-Dependence Measure) ---------------------------------------- The path-dependence $\mathcal{P}$ of a condensed object:
$$\mathcal{P}(x) = 1 - \frac{1}{|\Gamma(x)|}$$
where $|\Gamma(x)|$ is the number of distinct assembly pathways.
Properties: - $\mathcal{P} = 0$: Single pathway (no path-dependence) - $\mathcal{P} \rightarrow 1$: Many pathways (high path-dependence) - Relates to KID: $\mathcal{P}(x) \propto \frac{\sigma_{KID}}{\langle KID \rangle}$ (normalized variance of KID across pathways)
Theorem 6.3 (AI-KID Duality) ----------------------------- Assembly Index and KID are dual measures:
$$AI(x) \cdot KID(x) = \frac{I_{total}^2(x)}{I_{step} \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{form}}$$
Proof: ------ From Theorem 2.2: $AI = \frac{1}{I_{step}} \int KID \cdot V \, dt$
For constant KID over formation time: $$AI = \frac{KID \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{form}}{I_{step}}$$
Therefore: $$AI \cdot KID = \frac{KID^2 \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{form}}{I_{step}}$$
Since $I_{total} = KID \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{form}$: $$AI \cdot KID = \frac{I_{total}^2}{I_{step} \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{form}}$$ $\square$
math_axiom_report.toast_heuristic:
================================================================================ III. THE TOAST-HEURISTIC: FROM PRIGOGINE TO COMPLEXITY ================================================================================
Axiom 3.1 (Prigogine's Entropy Production) ------------------------------------------- For any dissipative structure, the total entropy production is:
$$\frac{dS_{total}}{dt} = \frac{dS_{system}}{dt} + \frac{dS_{environment}}{dt} \geq 0$$
where local entropy reduction in the system is compensated by entropy export to the environment.
Definition 3.1 (Dissipative Structure) -------------------------------------- A dissipative structure $\mathcal{D}$ is a self-organizing system that maintains its order through continuous energy/matter flow:
$$\mathcal{D} = \{X \in \mathcal{X} : \frac{dS_{system}}{dt} < 0 \text{ and } \frac{dS_{total}}{dt} \geq 0\}$$
Theorem 3.1 (Toast-Heuristic: Complexity from Dissipation) ----------------------------------------------------------- The complexity $C$ of a dissipative structure is proportional to its entropy production rate:
$$C(\mathcal{D}) = \alpha \cdot \frac{dS_{total}}{dt} \cdot \tau_{coherence}$$
where: - $\alpha$ is a system-specific constant - $\tau_{coherence}$ is the structural coherence time - $C$ measures "organized complexity"
Proof Derivation: ----------------- Step 1: Start with Prigogine's minimum entropy production principle $$\frac{dS_{total}}{dt} = \int_V \sigma \, dV$$ where $\sigma$ is the local entropy production density.
Step 2: For dissipative structures, order emerges when: $$\sigma_{internal} < 0 \text{ (local order)}$$ $$\sigma_{export} > |\sigma_{internal}| \text{ (compensating export)}$$
Step 3: The "amount" of structure is proportional to the exported entropy: $$S_{structure} \propto \int_0^{\tau} |\sigma_{internal}| \, dt$$
Step 4: Complexity requires both structure AND stability: $$C = S_{structure} \cdot f(\tau_{coherence})$$
where $f(\tau_{coherence})$ ensures only persistent structures count.
Step 5: For exponential decay of perturbations: $$f(\tau_{coherence}) = 1 - e^{-\tau_{coherence}/\tau_{char}}$$
Combining: $C \propto \frac{dS_{total}}{dt} \cdot \tau_{coherence}$ $\square$
Definition 3.2 (Toast Number $\mathcal{T}$) ------------------------------------------- The "Toast Number" quantifies the "brownedness" of a system - how much structure has emerged from dissipative processes:
$$\mathcal{T}(x) = \frac{KID(x)}{KID_{max}} \cdot \frac{\tau_{age}}{\tau_{relax}}$$
where: - $KID_{max}$ is the maximum possible KID for the system - $\tau_{age}$ is the system's age - $\tau_{relax}$ is the relaxation time to equilibrium
Interpretation: - $\mathcal{T} \ll 1$: "untoasted" (near equilibrium) - $\mathcal{T} \sim 1$: "golden brown" (optimal complexity) - $\mathcal{T} \gg 1$: "burnt" (over-constrained, fragile)
Theorem 3.2 (KID-Toast Relationship) ------------------------------------ For dissipative structures:
$$KID = \mathcal{T} \cdot \frac{dS_{total}}{dt} \cdot \frac{1}{k_B \ln 2 \cdot V_{eff}}$$
Proof: ------ From Definition 3.2: $\mathcal{T} = \frac{KID}{KID_{max}} \cdot \frac{\tau_{age}}{\tau_{relax}}$
For maximum KID (Landauer limit): $$KID_{max} = \frac{dS_{total}/dt}{k_B \ln 2 \cdot V_{eff}} \cdot \frac{\tau_{relax}}{\tau_{age}}$$
Solving for KID: $$KID = \mathcal{T} \cdot \frac{dS_{total}/dt}{k_B \ln 2 \cdot V_{eff}} \cdot \frac{\tau_{relax}}{\tau_{age}} \cdot \frac{\tau_{age}}{\tau_{relax}}$$
$$KID = \mathcal{T} \cdot \frac{dS_{total}}{dt} \cdot \frac{1}{k_B \ln 2 \cdot V_{eff}}$$ $\square$
Corollary 3.1 (Optimal Toast Condition) --------------------------------------- Maximum sustainable complexity occurs at:
$$\mathcal{T}_{optimal} = 1 \pm \delta$$
where $\delta$ is system-dependent (typically $\delta \approx 0.3$).
This corresponds to the "edge of chaos" - sufficient structure for complexity, sufficient flexibility for adaptation.
Theorem 6.4 (Complexity-Entropy Tradeoff) ----------------------------------------- There is a fundamental tradeoff between complexity and entropy:
$$C \cdot S_{local} = \text{constant} \cdot V_{eff}$$
where $C$ is complexity and $S_{local}$ is local entropy.
Proof: ------ 1. Complexity requires structure: $C \propto KID$ 2. Structure requires entropy export: $S_{export} \propto S_{local}$ 3. By Axiom A2: $S_{export} - S_{local} = \Delta S_{total} > 0$ 4. Therefore: $C \propto 1/S_{local}$ for fixed export capacity $\square$
math_axiom_report.boundary_conditions:
================================================================================ IV. BOUNDARY CONDITIONS AND CRITICAL THRESHOLDS ================================================================================
Axiom 4.1 (Minimum Condensation Principle) ------------------------------------------ Condensation occurs only when KID exceeds a critical threshold:
$$KID > KID_{crit} = \frac{k_B T \ln 2}{V_{min} \cdot \tau_{fluct}}$$
where: - $V_{min}$ is the minimum correlation volume - $\tau_{fluct}$ is the characteristic fluctuation timescale
Definition 4.1 (Condensation Threshold) --------------------------------------- The critical KID for phase transition to condensed state:
$$KID_{crit}(T) = \frac{k_B T \ln 2}{\xi^d \cdot \tau_{cor}}$$
where: - $\xi$ is the correlation length - $d$ is the spatial dimension - $\tau_{cor}$ is the correlation time
Theorem 4.1 (Phase Transition Condition) ---------------------------------------- A system undergoes ontological condensation when:
$$\frac{KID}{KID_{crit}} = \frac{\xi^d \cdot \tau_{cor} \cdot I_{causal}}{V_{interaction} \cdot k_B T \ln 2 \cdot \tau_{form}} > 1$$
Proof: ------ From Definition 4.1, condensation requires $KID > KID_{crit}$.
Substituting definitions: $$\frac{I_{causal}}{V_{interaction} \cdot \tau_{form}} > \frac{k_B T \ln 2}{\xi^d \cdot \tau_{cor}}$$
Rearranging: $$\frac{\xi^d \cdot \tau_{cor} \cdot I_{causal}}{V_{interaction} \cdot k_B T \ln 2 \cdot \tau_{form}} > 1$$
Define the condensation parameter: $$\mathcal{C} = \frac{\xi^d \cdot \tau_{cor} \cdot I_{causal}}{V_{interaction} \cdot k_B T \ln 2 \cdot \tau_{form}}$$
Condensation occurs when $\mathcal{C} > 1$. $\square$
Definition 4.2 (Self-Organized Criticality Parameter) ----------------------------------------------------- The SOC parameter $\mathcal{S}$ measures proximity to criticality:
$$\mathcal{S} = \frac{KID - KID_{crit}}{KID_{sat} - KID_{crit}}$$
where $KID_{sat}$ is the saturation KID (maximum stable value).
Interpretation: - $\mathcal{S} < 0$: Sub-critical (no condensation) - $\mathcal{S} = 0$: Critical threshold - $0 < \mathcal{S} < 1$: Super-critical (condensed) - $\mathcal{S} \rightarrow 1$: Saturation (fragile)
Theorem 4.2 (Bak's Sandpile Analogy) ------------------------------------ For self-organizing systems, the probability distribution of KID fluctuations follows a power law near criticality:
$$P(KID) \sim KID^{-\alpha}$$
where $\alpha \approx 1$ (universal for SOC systems).
Proof Sketch: ------------- 1. Near criticality, correlation length $\xi \rightarrow \infty$ 2. System becomes scale-invariant 3. No characteristic scale $\Rightarrow$ power-law statistics 4. Avalanche dynamics (Bak-Tang-Wiesenfeld model) applies 5. Therefore $P(KID) \sim KID^{-\alpha}$ with $\alpha \approx 1$ $\square$
Definition 4.3 (Emergence Threshold $\mathcal{E}$) -------------------------------------------------- The threshold for emergence of new ontological levels:
$$\mathcal{E}_n = n \cdot KID_{crit} \cdot \ln(AI_{min}^{(n)})$$
where: - $n$ is the ontological level (physical=1, chemical=2, biological=3, conscious=4) - $AI_{min}^{(n)}$ is the minimum Assembly Index for level $n$
Emergence Table: ---------------- | Level | Phenomenon | $AI_{min}$ | $\mathcal{E}_n$ (relative) | |-------|------------|------------|---------------------------| | 1 | Physical structures | 1 | 1.0 | | 2 | Chemical/molecular | 10 | 2.3 | | 3 | Biological/living | 100 | 4.6 | | 4 | Consciousness | 10,000 | 9.2 |
Theorem 4.3 (Emergence Criterion) --------------------------------- A new ontological level $n+1$ emerges when:
$$\int_{\Omega} KID \, dV \, dt \geq \mathcal{E}_{n+1}$$
AND
$$AI \geq AI_{min}^{(n+1)}$$
Proof: ------ Both conditions must be satisfied: 1. Sufficient KID accumulation (energy/information threshold) 2. Sufficient structural complexity (AI threshold)
The logarithmic relationship in $\mathcal{E}_n$ reflects the combinatorial explosion of possible configurations at higher levels. $\square$
Definition 4.4 (Stability Basin) -------------------------------- The stability basin $\mathcal{B}$ of a condensed state:
$$\mathcal{B} = \{(KID, AI) : KID_{min} < KID < KID_{max}, AI > AI_{crit}\}$$
where: - $KID_{min} = KID_{crit}$ (condensation threshold) - $KID_{max} = KID_{sat}$ (saturation/fragility threshold) - $AI_{crit}$ is the minimum AI for structural integrity
Theorem 6.1 (KID Conservation) ------------------------------ In a closed system, total KID is conserved:
$$\frac{d}{dt} \int_{\Omega} KID \, dV = 0$$
Proof: ------ 1. KID is information density: $KID = I/(V \cdot t)$ 2. Information is conserved in closed systems (Liouville theorem) 3. Therefore KID is conserved $\square$
Theorem 6.2 (KID Increase in Open Systems) ------------------------------------------- In open dissipative systems, local KID can increase:
$$\frac{d}{dt} \int_{\Omega_{local}} KID \, dV \geq 0$$
while global KID satisfies:
$$\frac{d}{dt} \int_{\Omega_{global}} KID \, dV \leq 0$$
Proof: ------ 1. Local entropy can decrease: $\frac{dS_{local}}{dt} < 0$ 2. Compensated by export: $\frac{dS_{export}}{dt} > |\frac{dS_{local}}{dt}|$ 3. Since $KID \propto -S$ (information = negative entropy): - Local KID increases - Global KID decreases $\square$
math_axiom_report.unified_framework:
================================================================================ V. UNIFIED FRAMEWORK: CONNECTIONS TO EXISTING THEORIES ================================================================================
V.1 KID AND INTEGRATED INFORMATION THEORY (IIT) --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Definition 5.1 (IIT's $\Phi$ - Integrated Information) -------------------------------------------------- Tononi's integrated information measures the "amount" of consciousness:
$$\Phi = \min_{\text{MIP}} \Phi^{MIP}$$
where MIP is the Minimum Information Partition.
Theorem 5.1 (KID-$\Phi$ Equivalence) -------------------------------- For conscious systems, KID and $\Phi$ are related by:
$$\Phi = KID \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{int} \cdot \eta_{integration}$$
where: - $\tau_{int}$ is the integration timescale - $\eta_{integration}$ is the integration efficiency (0 < $\eta$ < 1)
Proof: ------ 1. IIT defines $\Phi$ as irreducible, integrated information 2. KID measures information density per unit volume/time 3. Integration requires spatial and temporal coherence 4. Therefore: $\Phi = \int KID \cdot V_{eff} \, dt \cdot \eta_{integration}$ 5. For characteristic timescale $\tau_{int}$: $$\Phi = KID \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{int} \cdot \eta_{integration}$$ $\square$
Corollary 5.1 (Consciousness Threshold) --------------------------------------- Consciousness emerges when:
$$\Phi \geq \Phi_{min} \approx 0.5 \text{ bits}$$
which corresponds to:
$$KID \geq \frac{0.5}{V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{int} \cdot \eta_{integration}}$$
V.2 KID AND FREE ENERGY PRINCIPLE (FEP) --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Definition 5.2 (Free Energy - Friston) --------------------------------------- The variational free energy:
$$F[q] = \mathbb{E}_q[\ln q(\phi|y) - \ln p(\phi, y)]$$
where: - $q(\phi|y)$ is the recognition density (internal model) - $p(\phi, y)$ is the generative model
Theorem 5.2 (KID-Free Energy Relationship) ------------------------------------------ The free energy is the thermodynamic potential of KID:
$$F = k_B T \cdot KID \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{process}$$
Proof: ------ 1. Free energy bounds surprise: $F \geq -\ln p(y)$ 2. Surprise is proportional to information: $-\ln p(y) \propto I$ 3. KID is information density: $KID = I/(V \cdot t)$ 4. Therefore: $F \propto k_B T \cdot KID \cdot V \cdot t$ 5. For characteristic process time $\tau_{process}$: $$F = k_B T \cdot KID \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{process}$$ $\square$
Corollary 5.2 (Active Inference) -------------------------------- Active inference minimizes KID:
$$\frac{\delta KID}{\delta a} = 0$$
where $a$ represents actions. This is equivalent to minimizing free energy.
V.3 KID AND ASSEMBLY THEORY (AT) --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Definition 5.3 (Assembly Universe) ---------------------------------- The Assembly Universe $\mathcal{A}_U$ contains all possible objects:
$$\mathcal{A}_U = \{x : AI(x) < \infty\}$$
Theorem 5.3 (KID-Assembly Theory Unification) --------------------------------------------- The Assembly Index is the discrete integral of KID over the assembly pathway:
$$AI(x) = \left\lfloor \frac{1}{I_{step}} \int_{\gamma^*} KID \cdot V_{eff} \, dt \right\rfloor$$
where: - $I_{step}$ is the information per assembly step - $\gamma^*$ is the minimal pathway - $\lfloor \cdot \rfloor$ denotes the floor function
Proof: ------ 1. Each assembly step adds information $I_{step}$ 2. Total information: $I_{total} = AI(x) \cdot I_{step}$ 3. From KID definition: $I_{total} = \int KID \cdot V_{eff} \, dt$ 4. Therefore: $AI(x) = \frac{1}{I_{step}} \int KID \cdot V_{eff} \, dt$ 5. Since AI is integer-valued: floor function applies $\square$
V.4 KID AND VERLINDE'S ENTROPIC GRAVITY --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Definition 5.4 (Entropic Force) ------------------------------- Verlinde's entropic gravity:
$$F = T \nabla S$$
Theorem 5.4 (KID-Gravity Connection) ------------------------------------ Gravitational effects emerge from KID gradients:
$$\vec{F}_{grav} = \frac{\hbar c}{k_B} \cdot \nabla KID$$
Proof Sketch: ------------- 1. Entropic gravity: $F = T \nabla S$ 2. Shannon entropy: $S = k_B \ln 2 \cdot I$ 3. KID relates information to volume: $I = KID \cdot V \cdot t$ 4. For holographic screen: $V \propto A \cdot t$ (area-time) 5. Therefore: $\nabla S \propto \nabla KID$ 6. With appropriate constants: $F \propto \nabla KID$ $\square$
V.5 MASTER EQUATION: KID UNIFICATION --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Theorem 5.5 (KID Master Equation) ---------------------------------- All frameworks converge in the KID formalism:
$$KID = \frac{\Phi}{V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{int}} = \frac{F}{k_B T \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau} = \frac{AI \cdot I_{step}}{\int V_{eff} \, dt} = \frac{k_B}{\hbar c} \cdot \frac{F_{grav}}{\nabla V}$$
This establishes KID as the fundamental measure of ontological condensation.
Corollary 5.3 (Ontological Condensation Principle) -------------------------------------------------- All complex structures in the universe are manifestations of Kondensation-Information-Dichte exceeding critical thresholds:
$$\text{Complexity exists} \iff KID > KID_{crit}$$
This is the fundamental principle of the KID-Calculus.
================================================================================ VI. MEASUREMENT PROTOCOLS ================================================================================
Protocol M1: Measuring $I_{causal}$ ------------------------------------ 1. Record time series of system states: $\{X_t\}_{t=0}^{T}$ 2. Compute mutual information: $I(X_t; X_{t+\Delta t})$ 3. Estimate causal effect via intervention or Granger causality 4. Calculate: $I_{causal} = I(X_t; X_{t+\Delta t}) \cdot \eta_{cause}$
Protocol M2: Measuring $V_{interaction}$ ----------------------------------------- 1. Compute spatial correlation function: $C(r) = \langle X(0) X(r) \rangle$ 2. Define threshold: $\epsilon = e^{-1} \cdot C(0)$ 3. Integrate: $V_{interaction} = \int \Theta(|C(r)| - \epsilon) \, d^3r$
Protocol M3: Measuring KID --------------------------- 1. Measure $I_{causal}$ via Protocol M1 2. Measure $V_{interaction}$ via Protocol M2 3. Measure formation time $\tau_{form}$ 4. Calculate: $KID = \frac{I_{causal}}{V_{interaction} \cdot \tau_{form}}$
Protocol M4: Estimating AI from KID ------------------------------------ 1. Measure KID via Protocol M3 2. Estimate $V_{eff}$ and $\tau_{form}$ 3. Calculate: $AI \approx \frac{KID \cdot V_{eff} \cdot \tau_{form}}{k_B T \ln 2}$
